steps.title: Use table regression to avoid expansion mistakes
steps.content: The answer choices are quadratics, so evaluate $f(x)+g(x)$ at three easy $x$-values and let Desmos fit $ax^2+bx+c$. This is a fast way to recover the full equivalent quadratic without doing every FOIL step by hand.

steps.title: Read the coefficients and match the choice
steps.content:
The regression returns $a=4$, $b=-16$, and $c=39$, so
$$f(x)+g(x)=4x^2-16x+39$$
A quick hand check agrees: $(2x-5)^2=4x^2-20x+25$ and $2(2x+7)=4x+14$, which combine to $4x^2-16x+39$. Choice B is correct.
